1802	\begin{proof}
1803	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs, lem:base_change_mate_unit_value,
1804	        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor,
1805	        lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp,
1806	        def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
1807	  The concrete identity is the instantiation of the abstract variable-legs reindex
1808	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs} at the literal pullback projection legs
1809	  \(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\), \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\): with the codomain read
1810	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read} being definitionally the variable-legs read at
1811	  those legs, the concrete statement follows directly. The remainder of this proof records the
1812	  mathematical content of that abstract identity.
1813	
1814	  Throughout, let \(e : X' \xrightarrow{\sim} \operatorname{Spec}(A \otimes_R R')\) be the
1815	  canonical isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}, and
1816	  \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(\iota_{R'} : R' \to A \otimes_R R'\) the tensor
1817	  inclusions, so that by Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor} the two projection legs
1818	  factor as
1819	  \[
1820	    g' \;=\; e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A,
1821	    \qquad
1822	    f' \;=\; e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'} .
1823	  \]
1824	  The inner composite \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) is the four-factor map
1825	  \[
1826	    f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr) \;\circ\;
1827	    (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g', f})^{\mathrm{hom}} \;\circ\;
1828	    (\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}} \;\circ\;
1829	    (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f', g})^{\mathrm{inv}},
1830	  \]
1831	  with \(f = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\), \(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\),
1832	  \(\eta^{g'}\) the unit of the \((g')\)-adjunction, and
1833	  \(w : g' \circ f = f' \circ g\) the commutativity of the square.
1834	
1835	  \emph{Why a direct substitution fails.} The identity cannot be obtained by substituting the
1836	  leg factorizations directly, because the legs \(g'\) and \(f'\) appear in multiple positions
1837	  simultaneously: as the index of the adjunction \(\eta^{g'}\), inside the equality proof
1838	  \(w\) parametrizing \(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w\), inside the argument
1839	  \(f'_{*}\bigl((g')^{*}\widetilde M\bigr)\) of the codomain comparison
1840	  \(\gamma_{\psi}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), and inside the opaque codomain
1841	  identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} = \) Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
1842	  The reindex is therefore restructured into three sub-steps so that the legs become explicit
1843	  free variables; it is \emph{not} a single rewrite.
1844	
1845	  \emph{(i) Abstract variable-legs restatement.} Restate the codomain read — and, with it, the
1846	  whole four-factor chain — for \emph{generic} morphisms \(g', f'\) carrying the cone-leg
1847	  equalities \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and
1848	  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\) as explicit hypotheses, in place of the literal
1849	  projections \(\operatorname{pr}_1, \operatorname{pr}_2\). In this abstract form the legs are
1850	  free variables, not terms pinned by the pullback's universal property, so each can be
1851	  eliminated by substituting its defining equation: the equality proof \(w\), the adjunction
1852	  index, and the \(\gamma_{\psi}\)/\(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) arguments are all expressed in the
1853	  free leg variables and transform uniformly under the substitution. This restatement converts
1854	  the blocked rewrite into a legitimate one; it is the structural heart of the step.
1855	
1856	  \emph{(ii) \(\Gamma\)-collapse of the transparent coherences.} On global sections, read through
1857	  the \(\Gamma\)-pushforward dictionaries (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso},
1858	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), the two pushforward-composition factors and the
1859	  congruence factor are transparent. The composition coherences collapse to the identity —
1860	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) and
1861	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f',g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) evaluate, on the top open, to the identity
1862	  map on restriction of scalars (their section-level values are the identity) — and they
1863	  serve only to rebracket the iterated pushforward along the two factorizations
1864	  \((g' \circ f)_* = (f' \circ g)_*\) of the square's composite. (The
1865	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) collapse is a transparent re-association
1866	  that is most naturally accounted for together with the leg-reindex of step~(iii) rather than at
1867	  this point; the \(\Gamma\)-collapse identity itself is the same.) The congruence factor collapses
1868	  to a canonical transport along the equality of opens: its section-level value
1869	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}}\) is the
1870	  presheaf-restriction along the canonical equality of opens, i.e.\ a repackaging along
1871	  that equality carrying no substantive content. Thus after the \(\Gamma\)-collapse the only
1872	  surviving non-trivial factor is \(f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr)\) read on global
1873	  sections.
1874	
1875	  \emph{(iii) Reduction to Seam~1 via the leg-reindex engine.} With the legs substituted
1876	  (step~(i)) to \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), the leg-reindex engine
1877	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}, instantiated at \(a = e\),
1878	  \(b = \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), \(N = \widetilde M\), rewrites the unit factor
1879	  \(\eta^{g'} = \eta^{e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}\), together with the
1880	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})\) coherence, into the affine
1881	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit followed by \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\) of the
1882	  \(e\)-unit. Since \(e\) is an isomorphism of schemes, its pullback functor is an equivalence
1883	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}) and the \(e\)-unit is invertible, hence an
1884	  isomorphism absorbed into the codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\). The surviving
1885	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit has section value over \(\operatorname{Spec} A\) equal to
1886	  the algebraic unit \(\eta_M : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of Seam~1
1887	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}). Composing the surviving factor with the
1888	  codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
1889	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) and reading over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by
1890	  restriction of scalars along \(\psi\) identifies all four factors with the restriction of
1891	  scalars along \(\psi\) of the codomain-read transport of the unit value, namely
1892	  \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\)
1893	  (Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}) — the
1894	  ``\(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\), \(\mathcal F'\) is
1895	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\)'' bookkeeping of the source. Each of (i)--(iii) extracts as a
1896	  named lemma; together they close the reindex without ever rewriting the bare legs.
1897	\end{proof}

1898	isomorphism `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` (in the orientation
1899	`Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, `r' ⊗ m ↦ (r' ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`); since `cancelBaseChange` carries no

1916	      (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)

1942	/-- **Affine-local compatibility of the base-change map (locality reduction).** For `f` affine
1943	and `F` quasi-coherent, the base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism as soon
1944	as it restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of the base `S'`. This is
1945	the locality reduction underlying `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`: it discharges the
1946	affine-open locality criterion `Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens` for the base-change map,
1947	the per-affine-open hypothesis being the affine–affine section assertion supplied (over each chart)
1948	by the section-level computation. The `[IsAffineHom f]`/`[F.IsQuasicoherent]` hypotheses are
1949	carried because the per-open assertion `H` is only available under them (they make `X` restrict to
1950	`Spec A` and `F` to `M^~` over each affine chart); the reduction itself is the locality criterion.
1951	See blueprint `lem:base_change_map_affine_local`. -/
1952	theorem base_change_map_affine_local (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
1953	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
1954	    (H : ∀ U : S'.affineOpens, IsIso ((pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F).app U)) :
1955	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) :=
1956	  (Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F)).mpr H
1957	
1958	/-- **Affine base change.** If `f` is an affine morphism and the square is
1959	cartesian, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an isomorphism. In the
1960	affine-local picture this is the associativity isomorphism
1961	`(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M ≅ R' ⊗_R M`, which needs no flatness.

1964	theorem affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
1965	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
1966	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by

1968	  -- that the base-change map is an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of `S'`.

1970	  -- section assertion that `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` is intended to supply.

1972	  intro U
1973	  -- Remaining goal: `IsIso (Hom.app (pushforwardBaseChangeMap …) U)` for `U` affine.
1974	  --
1975	  -- The two obligations of the blueprint affine close are now NAMED declarations:
1976	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL identification is `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`
1977	  --     (above): in the affine–affine model `Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, hence an iso (no
1978	  --     flatness). Its statement is in place; its proof (the 4-step generator trace) is the
1979	  --     genuine crux still carrying a `sorry`.
1980	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL locality reduction is `base_change_map_affine_local` (just applied):
1981	  --     it reduced the global iso to this per-affine-open `U` goal.
1982	  --
1983	  -- WHAT REMAINS HERE (the AFFINE REDUCTION, "obligation 1"): the ambient `S, S', X, X'` are
1984	  -- ARBITRARY (only `f` affine, `F` quasi-coherent). To discharge the per-`U` goal one must
1985	  -- restrict the cartesian square over the affine open `U = Spec R' ⊆ S'` and a chosen affine
1986	  -- `Spec R ⊆ S` containing `g(U)` — over which `[IsAffineHom f]` makes `X` restrict to `Spec A`
1987	  -- and `[F.IsQuasicoherent]` makes `F` restrict to `M^~` — and IDENTIFY `(pushforwardBaseChangeMap
1988	  -- …).app U` with the affine–affine base-change map of the restricted square (blueprint Step 2:

1992	  -- compatibility of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is itself Mathlib-absent and is the remaining

2004	theorem flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [Flat g]
2005	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
2006	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by

2008	  -- the statement is local on `S'`, so reduce to `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`
2009	  -- with `A → B` flat.  Choose a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X`.  Since `f`
2010	  -- is quasi-compact and quasi-separated the Čech complex of `𝒰` computes
2011	  -- `H⁰(X, F)`, and base change identifies `Čech(𝒰_B, F_B) ≅ Čech(𝒰, F) ⊗_A B`
2012	  -- term by term via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`.  Flatness of `A → B`
2013	  -- makes `- ⊗_A B` exact, so it commutes with `H⁰`, giving the isomorphism
2014	  -- `H⁰(X, F) ⊗_A B ≅ H⁰(X_B, F_B)`.  Needs the (missing) Čech-cohomology /
2015	  -- affine-cover infrastructure for `SheafOfModules`; see

2003	  Let \(e : X \to Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes and \(N\) a \(Y\)-module. Then the unit

2014	  left adjoint is an equivalence is an isomorphism; hence \(\eta^{e}_{N}\) is invertible.

2301	  (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
2302	  Let \(\rho : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\), be the
2303	  inner comparison value of Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}. The extension of
2304	  scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of \(\rho\), post-composed with the algebraic counit
2305	  \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) of the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\psi\),
2306	  is an \(R'\)-linear map \(R' \otimes_R M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\); on the generator
2307	  \(r' \otimes m\) it returns \(r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\),
2308	  which is exactly the value of the inverse regrouping isomorphism
2309	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on \(r' \otimes m\). Both maps being
2310	  \(R'\)-linear and agreeing on the generators \(r' \otimes m\), they coincide:
2311	  \[
2312	    \operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho) \,\circ\, \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}
2313	      \;=\; \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}.
2314	  \]

2316	\begin{proof}

2318	  Both sides are \(R'\)-linear maps out of \(R' \otimes_R M\), so by extensionality it suffices to
2319	  check the value on the generators \(r' \otimes m\). The left side sends
2320	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \rho(m) = r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) =
2321	  (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\), using that \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) reads the extension of
2322	  scalars by acting through the \(R'\)-scalar. The inverse regrouping isomorphism of
2323	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} sends, by definition,
2324	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\) (in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation). The two
2325	  values agree on every generator, so the maps are equal. No flatness hypothesis is used.
2326	\end{proof}

2719	  conjugate of \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) by the two dictionary isomorphisms
2720	  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}, \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\), it follows that
2721	  \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is itself an isomorphism. This closes the affine case
2722	  \emph{directly} on global sections, with no separate abstract-mate-versus-concrete-map
2723	  identification at the level of sheaves. (The full equality
2724	  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\alpha) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1}
2725	  = \operatorname{cancelBaseChange}^{-1}\) of the intended signature follows from the
2726	  same chain once \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}, \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) are formed; the
2727	  formalized decl records the \(\mathrm{IsIso}\ \Gamma(\alpha)\) corollary, which is
2728	  what the affine close consumes.)
2729	\end{proof}

2731	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2732	[Affine base change]
2733	  \label{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}
2734	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso}
2735	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_map_affine_local,
2736	        lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso,
2737	        lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange,
2738	        lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
2739	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
2740	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I)
2741	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L906--918).
2742	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
2743	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
2744	  % Assume $f$ is affine.
2745	  % In this case $f_*\mathcal{F} \cong Rf_*\mathcal{F}$ is
2746	  % a quasi-coherent sheaf, and for every base change diagram
2747	  % (\ref{equation-base-change-diagram})
2748	  % we have
2749	  % $$
2750	  % g^*f_*\mathcal{F} = f'_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}.

2752	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
2753	  change''.}
2754	  Assume \(f : X \to S\) is an affine morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a
2755	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then for every base-change square as
2756	  above the canonical map of
2757	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism
2758	  \[
2759	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\cong\; f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr).
2760	  \]
2761	  The quasi-coherence hypothesis on \(\mathcal{F}\) is essential and is not a mere
2762	  convenience: the proof works over an affine open by writing
2763	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) for a module \(M\), and a general
2764	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module need not be of this form
2765	  \(\widetilde{M}\); without it neither the affine description of the pushforward
2766	  nor the tensor-associativity computation below is available, and the conclusion
2767	  fails.
2768	\end{lemma}

2771	% Lemma \ref{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.
2772	% The statement is local on $S$ and $S'$. Hence we may
2773	% assume $X = \Spec(A)$, $S = \Spec(R)$,
2774	% $S' = \Spec(R')$ and $\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}$
2775	% for some $A$-module $M$.

2778	% Namely, $X' = \Spec(R' \otimes_R A)$ and

2781	% Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the

2783	% $$

2786	% as $R'$-modules."
2787	% NOTE: This affine lemma is closed directly on global sections, via the two named
2788	% obligations: lem:base_change_map_affine_local (locality reduction on the affine
2789	% opens of S') and lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange (the section-
2790	% level value of the base-change map is cancelBaseChange). Both proved affine tilde
2791	% dictionaries -- lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso and lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso --
2792	% feed the second obligation. cancelBaseChange is in Mathlib with no flatness; the
2793	% affine case carries no flatness hypothesis. No Čech infrastructure is used here.
2794	\begin{proof}
2795	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:base_change_map_affine_local, lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}
2796	  The proof is the composite of the two named obligations, taken \emph{directly on
2797	  global sections}.
2798	
2799	  \medskip
2800	  \emph{First reduction (locality on \(S'\)).} By
2801	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} it suffices to prove that the
2802	  base-change map of Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an
2803	  isomorphism on sections over every affine open of \(S'\). This is exactly the
2804	  locality reduction Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_map_affine_local}: restricting the
2805	  cartesian square to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R') \subseteq S'\) and
2806	  a chosen affine \(\operatorname{Spec}(R) \subseteq S\) containing \(g(U)\), the
2807	  hypothesis \([\mathrm{IsAffineHom}\ f]\) makes \(X\) restrict to
2808	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) makes it restrict
2809	  to \(\widetilde{M}\) for an \(A\)-module \(M\). (It is here that quasi-coherence is
2810	  used: it is what licenses writing \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\).) Computing the
2811	  fibre product gives \(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\) and
2812	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) the tilde of the \((R' \otimes_R A)\)-module
2813	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\). The matter is thus reduced to the affine--affine
2814	  model, where the base-change map is a morphism \(\alpha\) of quasi-coherent
2815	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R')\)-modules.
2816	
2817	  \medskip
2818	  \emph{Passage to global sections.} The functor
2819	  \(\widetilde{(-)} : \operatorname{ModuleCat}(R') \to
2820	  (\operatorname{Spec} R').\mathrm{Modules}\) is fully faithful with essential image
2821	  the quasi-coherent modules; equivalently the counit
2822	  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{N})} \to \mathcal{N}\) is an isomorphism exactly when
2823	  \(\mathcal{N}\) is quasi-coherent. Source and target of \(\alpha\) are
2824	  quasi-coherent, so both counits are isomorphisms and, by their naturality,
2825	  \[
2826	    \mathrm{IsIso}\,\alpha
2827	    \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
2828	    \mathrm{IsIso}\bigl(\Gamma(\alpha)\bigr),
2829	  \]
2830	  where \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is a concrete \(R'\)-linear map in
2831	  \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\). The whole computation therefore stays at the level
2832	  of modules.
2833	
2834	  \medskip
2835	  \emph{Section-level identification (the second obligation).} By the section-level
2836	  computation Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}, the map
2837	  \(\Gamma(\alpha)\), with its domain \(\Gamma(g^*(f_*\widetilde{M}))\) identified with
2838	  \(R' \otimes_R M\) and its codomain \(\Gamma(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde{M})\) identified
2839	  with \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) through the two dictionaries
2840	  Lemmas~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} and~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso},
2841	  \emph{is} the canonical cancellation isomorphism
2842	  \[
2843	    \operatorname{cancelBaseChange} :
2844	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M,
2845	    \qquad (r' \otimes a) \otimes m \;\mapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m)
2846	  \]
2847	  of \(R'\)-modules (Lemma~\ref{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}). This is the entire
2848	  content of the affine close, and it is performed \emph{directly on the module of
2849	  global sections} — there is no separate abstract adjoint-mate identification at the
2850	  level of sheaves. Because \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) is an isomorphism with
2851	  no flatness hypothesis whatsoever, \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is an isomorphism, hence so is
2852	  \(\alpha\) on the affine chart, and by the locality reduction so is the global
2853	  base-change map. Flatness of \(g\) plays no role in the affine lemma; it enters only
2854	  in the global statement Theorem~\ref{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}, where it is
2855	  used to commute \(- \otimes_A B\) with the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\).
2856	\end{proof}

2858	\section{Flat base change for the pushforward}
2859	
2860	The global statement is assembled from the affine lemma and the single homological
2861	input that flatness preserves the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\). That input is
2862	supplied verbatim by Mathlib, recorded here as a dependency anchor.
2863	
2864	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change preserves finite equalizers]
2865	  \label{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
2866	  \lean{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}
2867	  \mathlibok
2868	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
2869	  Let \(A \to B\) be a flat ring homomorphism (more generally, let \(B\) be a flat
2870	  \(A\)-module that is also an \(A\)-algebra, with the scalar-tower compatibility),
2871	  and let \(f, g : N \rightrightarrows P\) be a pair of \(A\)-linear maps. Write
2872	  \(\operatorname{eq}(f, g) = \{x \in N : f(x) = g(x)\}\) for their equalizer. Then the
2873	  canonical \(B\)-linear comparison map
2874	  \[
2875	    B \otimes_A \operatorname{eq}(f, g)
2876	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
2877	    \operatorname{eq}\bigl(\mathbf{1}_B \otimes f,\ \mathbf{1}_B \otimes g\bigr)
2878	  \]
2879	  is an isomorphism; that is, base change along a flat algebra commutes with the
2880	  formation of the finite equalizer (equivalently, of the kernel: the case
2881	  \(g = 0\) is \texttt{LinearMap.tensorKerEquiv}, underlain by
2882	  \texttt{Module.Flat.ker\_lTensor\_eq} and \texttt{Module.Flat.eqLocus\_lTensor\_eq}).
2883	  Since \(- \otimes_A B\) also preserves finite products, it follows that
2884	  \(- \otimes_A B\) carries the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) equalizer
2885	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows
2886	    \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})\) of a finite cover to the corresponding
2887	  equalizer of the base-changed cover.
2888	\end{lemma}

2890	\begin{theorem}\leanok
2891	[Flat base change, $i=0$ case]
2892	  \label{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}
2893	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso}
2894	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward,
2895	        lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
2896	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 02KH (Lemma "Flat base change", part for
2897	  % $i = 0$) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L947--970, and
2898	  % references/stacks-coherent.md).
2899	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
2900	  % $$
2901	  % \xymatrix{
2902	  % X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
2903	  % S' \ar[r]^g & S
2904	  % }
2905	  % $$
2906	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
2907	  % with pullback $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$.
2908	  % Assume that $g$ is flat and that $f$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
2909	  % For any $i \geq 0$
2910	  % \begin{enumerate}
2911	  % \item the base change map of
2912	  % Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-base-change-map-flat-case}
2913	  % is an isomorphism
2914	  % $$
2915	  % g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
2916	  % $$
2917	  % \item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
2918	  % $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
2919	  % \end{enumerate}"
2920	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH (``Flat base change''), the
2921	  \(i = 0\) case.}
2922	  Consider the cartesian square
2923	  \[
2924	    \begin{array}{ccc}
2925	      X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
2926	      {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
2927	      S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
2928	    \end{array}
2929	  \]
2930	  with \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and
2931	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). Assume that \(g\) is flat and that \(f\)
2932	  is quasi-compact and quasi-separated. Then the base-change map of
2933	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism
2934	  \[
2935	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2936	    f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr) = f'_*\mathcal{F}' .
2937	  \]
2938	  Equivalently, in the affine situation \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
2939	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat, the comparison map
2940	  \[
2941	    H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2942	    H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
2943	  \]
2944	  is an isomorphism, where \(X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)}
2945	  \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) and \(\mathcal{F}_B\) is the pullback of
2946	  \(\mathcal{F}\).
2947	
2948	  The hypothesis that \(\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent is essential: it is what
2949	  makes \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent on the affine base and thus the tilde of
2950	  a module, and it is what lets the affine reduction
2951	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}) write \(\mathcal{F}\) as
2952	  \(\widetilde{M}\) over each affine open. Without it the comparison map need not be
2953	  an isomorphism.
2954	\end{theorem}

2999	% $$

3002	% $$

3014	% NOTE: For the $i = 0$ case the argument is Čech-free: $H^0(X,\mathcal{F})$ is the
3015	% sheaf-condition equalizer of a finite affine cover (Čech degree 0/1, NOT Čech
3016	% cohomology -- no Leray, no spectral sequence), each term base-changes by the affine
3017	% lemma lem:affine_base_change_pushforward, and flatness of $A \to B$ commutes

3020	% Mayer--Vietoris induction on the number of cover members named in the source quote.
3021	\begin{proof}
3022	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward, lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
3023	  For \(i = 0\) the argument is {\v C}ech-\emph{free}: it uses only the sheaf
3024	  condition (the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) part of {\v C}ech, i.e.\ an equalizer of a finite
3025	  cover), the affine lemma, and the fact that flat base change commutes with that
3026	  finite equalizer. There is no Leray sequence and no {\v C}ech-to-cohomology spectral
3027	  sequence.
3028	
3029	  \medskip
3030	  \emph{Reduction to the affine target.} The statement is local on \(S'\), so we
3031	  may assume \(S\) and \(S'\) are affine, say \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and
3032	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat. Because \(f\) is
3033	  quasi-compact and quasi-separated, \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent on
3034	  \(S\); it is therefore the sheaf associated to the \(A\)-module
3035	  \(H^0(S, f_*\mathcal{F}) = H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\). Likewise
3036	  \(f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) is the sheaf on \(S'\) associated to the \(B\)-module
3037	  \(H^0(X', \mathcal{F}') = \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\). Since pullback along \(g\)
3038	  corresponds to \(- \otimes_A B\) on modules, the base-change map becomes the
3039	  comparison map of \(B\)-modules
3040	  \[
3041	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B),
3042	    \qquad X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)} \operatorname{Spec}(B),
3043	  \]
3044	  and it suffices to prove that this map is an isomorphism.
3045	
3046	  \medskip
3047	  \emph{\(H^0\) as a finite equalizer (the sheaf condition).} Choose a finite affine
3048	  open cover \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\) (possible since \(X\) is
3049	  quasi-compact). The sheaf condition for \(\mathcal{F}\) on this cover presents the
3050	  global sections as the equalizer of the two restriction maps
3051	  \[
3052	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})
3053	    \;=\;
3054	    \operatorname{eq}\Bigl(
3055	      \textstyle\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F})
3056	      \;\rightrightarrows\;
3057	      \textstyle\prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})
3058	    \Bigr),
3059	    \qquad U_{ij} = U_i \cap U_j,
3060	  \]
3061	  where the two parallel maps are \(s \mapsto (s_i|_{U_{ij}})\) and
3062	  \(s \mapsto (s_j|_{U_{ij}})\). This is precisely Čech degree \(0\) into degree
3063	  \(1\); it is the sheaf axiom and requires \emph{no} separatedness of \(X\) and no
3064	  higher cohomology.
3065	
3066	  \medskip
3067	  \emph{The separated case.} Suppose first that \(X\) is separated over \(A\). Then
3068	  every intersection \(U_{ij}\) is affine. Base changing the cover yields a finite
3069	  affine cover \(\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \dots \cup (U_t)_B\) of \(X_B\),
3070	  and the same sheaf-condition equalizer presents \(\Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\). The
3071	  affine lemma Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward} (in its
3072	  \(i = 0\)/global-sections form) identifies each base-changed term with the original
3073	  term tensored over \(A\) with \(B\):
3074	  \[
3075	    \Gamma\bigl((U_i)_B, \mathcal{F}_B\bigr) \cong \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B,
3076	    \qquad
3077	    \Gamma\bigl((U_{ij})_B, \mathcal{F}_B\bigr) \cong \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B,
3078	  \]
3079	  compatibly with the two restriction maps. Hence the equalizer diagram for \(X_B\) is
3080	  the diagram for \(X\) with \(- \otimes_A B\) applied (using that \(- \otimes_A B\)
3081	  commutes with the finite products \(\prod_i\), \(\prod_{i,j}\)). Because \(A \to B\)
3082	  is flat, base change commutes with the formation of this finite equalizer
3083	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}):
3084	  \[
3085	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B
3086	    = \operatorname{eq}(\cdots) \otimes_A B
3087	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
3088	    \operatorname{eq}(\cdots \otimes_A B)
3089	    = \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B).
3090	  \]
3091	  This is exactly the comparison map of the reduction step, so it is an isomorphism.
3092	
3093	  \medskip
3094	  \emph{The quasi-separated case.} In general \(X\) need only be quasi-separated. We
3095	  argue by Mayer--Vietoris induction on the number \(t\) of members of a finite affine
3096	  cover \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\), each step reducing to the
3097	  separated/affine case. For \(t = 1\), \(X = U_1\) is affine and the claim is the

3099	  \(W = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_{t-1}\) (an open covered by \(t - 1\) affines). Their
